_note: About page content for one locale. The sibling .<other>.json holds the translation. The public repositories list is derived from papers.json.
lede: The site itself is open source, published via GitHub Pages. The entire site is cloneable and adaptable. MIT license with explicit disclaimers: not responsible for decisions or costs incurred by adopters; shared in the spirit of national collaboration; users must evaluate and assess these tools in their own context.

use.heading: How to use this site
paras: Sequential reading is the recommended path. Each paper builds on what comes before. Lateral links between papers let readers jump sideways without losing context.

Every paper is downloadable as Markdown and JSON so readers can feed them into their own AI tooling. The full site is cloneable from GitHub. The site uses a small cookie to track which papers a reader has visited and surface what is new since their last visit.
No vendor or product is being advocated. For transparency, any vendors or models actually used in production will be disclosed.

cards.title: Where to talk about the work
